fact: July 20 is exactly day 201 of the year, which means there are only 164 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun blazes down for about 15 hours a day in many places!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time of year, making that crazy buzzing sound that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
Why do fireflies blink so much in mid-July? They're having their biggest dating season of the year, flashing secret love codes to each other!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 20 falls smack in the middle of winter, with only about 9 hours of daylight.
Most sunflowers reach their full giant size around this date, with their heavy heads starting to droop and follow the sun across the sky like slow-motion dancers.
The zodiac switches from Cancer to Leo right around July 20-23, marking the transition from crabby water energy to fiery lion power!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying peak in many regions during late July - a single female can bite you up to 20 times in one night!
Ocean water temperatures hit their warmest point of the summer around this time in the Northern Hemisphere, perfect for jellyfish blooms.
In northern countries like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around July 20, creating an eerie twilight that lasts all night long.
Corn plants grow so fast in mid-July heat that you can literally hear them creaking and popping as they stretch upward - some grow 4 inches in a single day!
The summer triangle of stars (made by Vega, Altair, and Deneb) appears directly overhead around midnight on July 20 in the Northern Hemisphere.
